explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to textual descriptions of **acceptance, rejection, denial, abandonment, or quitting** (such as a person or organization accepting a charge, ignoring evidence, quitting a party, denying a request, or abandoning a place).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across political, legal, social, and personal narratives, including reports on resignations, legal plea deals, ignored evidence, denied permissions, and abandoned projects. It is present primarily in English but also in multilingual snippets (e.g., Russian, Spanish) when the semantic content involves such actions, indicating cross-linguistic sensitivity to the concept.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: Low-activation texts are neutral, factual statements—often truncated news leads, scientific notes, or simple announcements—that lack any overt semantic framing of acceptance, rejection, or termination. They avoid the decisive actions or contentious decisions that characterize the high-activation examples.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects events or statements where an entity actively embraces, spurns, or ends something, serving as a feature for tracking decision-making and conflict in narratives.

results.plain_text: The NCAA replaced the Ratings Percentage Index, or RPI, with the NCAA Evaluation Tool. The NCAA Tournament selection committee utilized the NET to assess wins, losses, schedule strength, game site and offensive and defensive efficiency. It was trumpeted as a more comprehensive measurement of the 343 Division I programs. However, the NCAA never insisted that this season’s tournament selection process would be nothing but NET. Clemson and Furman were ranked No. 35 and No. 41, respectively, in the most recent NET standings. Nevertheless, the selection committee passed over the Tigers and the Paladins to fill the 68-team bracket. Coaches, players and fans from both teams waited for days on that elusive invitation. They watched Sunday as those 36 at-large slots were granted to other bubble teams. The last four teams selected were Belmont, Temple, Arizona State and St. John’s, whose were ranked Nos. 47, 56, 63 and 73 in the NET standings. Furman and Clemson must wait for an explanation on the NET rankings’ diminished influence, but they did not have to wait long for a consolation prize. They accepted bids to the National Invitation Tournament. Clemson earned a No. 2 seed and will host its first-round game against Wright State in Littlejohn Coliseum. Clemson coach Brad Brownell coached at Wright State before arriving in Clemson. Clemson will make its 17th appearance in the NIT. The Tigers last competed in the tournament in 2017. They lost in the first round that year. Clemson advanced to the semifinals in 2014. Furman earned a No. 3 seed and will host its first-round game against No. 6 Wichita State in Timmons Arena. Furman competed in the NIT only once previously. The Paladins fell to West Virginia in the first round in 1991. Teams will play on campus sites until the semifinals in Madison Square Garden in New York City. Clemson closed the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament with a 19-13 overall record after a calamitous loss to North Carolina State. Clemson squandered an 18-point first-half lead which granted N.C. State the opportunity to seize victory on a questionable foul call. Furman holds a 25-7 overall record after falling to UNC-Greensboro in the semifinal round of the Southern Conference Tournament. UNC-Greensboro was named an No. 1 seed in the NIT and will host No. 8 seed Campbell in the first round. SoCon regular-season and tournament champion Wofford earned the No. 7 seed in the Midwest Region of the NCAA Tournament. The Terriers will open against No. 10-seed Seton Hall in Jacksonville. South Carolina was ranked No. 81 in the NET standings. The Gamecocks closed Southeastern Conference Tournament with a 16-16 overall record after falling to eventual champion Auburn in the quarterfinals.The Gamecocks were not invited to the NIT. The happiest team on Selection Sunday may be the City of Columbia. The Capital City will host first and second round games at Colonial Life Arena, and it drew an enticing collection of teams with passionate, well-traveled fan bases. ACC Tournament champion Duke earned the No. 1 overall seed in the NCAA Tournament. The Blue Devils will open the tournament in Columbia as part of the East Region against the winner of the North Carolina Central and North Dakota State play-in game. Virginia Commonwealth, the No. 8 seed in the East Region will meet No. 9 seed Central Florida in Columbia. Virginia earned the No. 1 seed in the South Region. The Cavaliers will open against Gardner-Webb in Columbia. Ole Miss, the No. 8 seed in the South Region, will open against No. 9 seed Oklahoma in Columbia.

results.actual_text: in schools; Illinois Supreme Court denies governor's appeal The Illinois Supreme Court on Friday denied an appeal from Gov. JB Pritzker in the legal fight over COVID-
results.plain_text: No more masks in schools; Illinois Supreme Court denies governor's appeal The Illinois Supreme Court on Friday denied an appeal from Gov. JB Pritzker in the legal fight over COVID-19 restrictions in schools. In response, Pritzker indicated that the state will lift school mask requirements in the coming days. "We will recommend that all school districts follow CDC guidance and will update our existing guidance in the coming days," said Pritzker. The lawsuits involved in the Supreme Court order centered on the state's ability to impose vaccination rules for teachers, mask requirements and "exclusion" — the practice of barring students or staff from school buildings after a COVID-19 exposure. The rules from the governor, Illinois Department of Public Health, and Illinois State Board of Education were challenged in several lawsuits filed on behalf of teachers and parents in about 170 of the state's 853 school districts. The court outright denied Pritzker's appeal and vacated a temporary restraining order issued by Judge Raylene Griwchow in the Sangamon County Circuit Court. The temporary restraining order barred the school districts from enforcing mask requirements and other COVID-19 mitigations without "due process" and voided the state's rules. The governor said he appreciates that the court vacated the restraining order. He said this gives him the authority to impose a mask mandate should it be required in the future. The court remanded the matter back to Sangamon County Circuit Court. Two of the court's three Republican jurists, Justices Michael Burke and David Overstreet, dissented from the order. Illinois' mask mandate:State mask mandate ends on Monday. Here's what you need to know Also on Friday, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention eased mask recommendations advising masking only in counties where healthcare systems are highly strained. The CDC has adopted a new metric called "COVID-19 Community Levels" which is determined through hospital beds used, hospital admissions and new cases. The CDC classifies most counties in Illinois, including Sangamon County, as having low or medium levels as of Feb. 24.

results.actual_text: visitado en Chapultepec Un extraño reptil que fue encontrado abandonado y rescatado en los canales de la alcaldía Xoch
results.plain_text:
Extraño reptil rescatado en Xochimilco puede ser visitado en Chapultepec  Un extraño reptil que fue encontrado abandonado y rescatado en los canales de la alcaldía Xochimilco de la Ciudad de México ya puede ser visitado en Chapultepec  En agosto del año pasado personal de la Brigada de Vigilancia Animal rescataron a un extraño reptil en los canales de la alcaldía Xochimilco; este animal mide 120 centímetros de largo aproximadamente que causó alerta entre los habitantes de la zona y vistantes pues había reportes de lo que parecía ser un caimán, sin embargo al encontrarlo se confirmó que se trataba de un ejemplar de varano del Nilo. Un vez rescatado, el extraño reptil fue trasladado a las instalaciones de la Brigada donde estuvo resguardado y en observación para hace unos días ser reubicado en el Zoológico de Chapultepec en donde ya se le puede visitar. ¿Qué es un varano del Nilo?    Este extraño reptil es una especie que puede medir hasta metro y medio y pesar cerca de 15 kilos, se alimenta de peces, crustáceos, mamíferos, pájaros, lagartijas, insectos, huevos entre otras cosas y debido al estado en el que fue encontrado en 
                Xochimilco
            
, este ejemplar fue sometido a una dieta formulada por los especialistas para recuperarse de la desnutrición que presentaba y algunas deformidades óseas que se le encontraron.  A este animalito lo puedes visitar en la zona del Herpertario del Zoológico de Chapultepec ya que es considerada un especie exótica y es ahí dónde el público puede conocer más sobre ellos. El recinto tiene un horario de martes a domingo de 9 de la mañana a 5 de la tarde y además de al varano del Nilo, también puedes disfrutar de otros animales que se encuentran ahí para su preservación. adn40, siempre conmigo. Suscríbete a nuestro canal de Telegram y lleva la información en tus manos.  LO MÁS VISTO  
                        
                            
                                
                                     ¿Perdiste tu licencia de conducir permanente? Te decimos cómo la puedes renovar en la CDMX, cuáles son los documentos que se te piden y su costo.  
                        
                            
                                
                                     Aquí en adn40 te explicamos cómo funcionará la tecnología para emitir la alerta sísmica en los teléfonos celulares así como cuándo se implementará.  
                        
                            
                                
                                     Durante los trabajos de una obra de drenaje en Río San Joaquín ocurrió un derrumbe que dejó a 4 trabajadores atrapados, uno de ellos murió en el lugar.  
                        
                            
                                
                                     Los trabajos de remodelación de la Linea 1 del Metro CDMX están por terminar y será cuestión de semanas para abrir tramo cerrado y comience la segunda etapa.  
                        
                            
                                
                                     El Metro CDMX cierra la estación Zócalo-Tenochtitlan hasta nuevo aviso; estas son las paradas alternativas que podrán utilizar los usuarios del transporte.  
                        
                            
                                
                                     ¡Toma precauciones! Las manifestaciones hoy viernes 20 de enero podrían significar contratiempos, conoce en qué alcaldías de la CDMX se llevarán a cabo.

results.plain_text: BRATISLAVA, SLOVAKIA (BLOOMBERG) - The European nation that protected its population better than any other from Covid-19 at the start of the global pandemic is now trying to do one better - by testing its entire population for the virus.  Slovakia, a country of 5.5 million people on the European Union's eastern border, is preparing to launch a two-stage process using cheap antibody tests to help stop the resurgence in new cases that's sweeping across the continent.  Others have shown success in mass testing, with China canvassing cities with populations far exceeding Slovakia's and Taiwan not registering a domestic case in more than 200 days. But none has yet tried to test all of its citizens.  Encouraged by turnout of more than 90 per cent at a pilot round last weekend, Prime Minister Igor Matovic has vowed to make Slovakia the world's first country to know the full scope of contagion in its borders.  He has pushed the idea as an alternative to reimposing measures far harsher than in the spring, when lockdowns wiped out more than 10 per cent of most European economies.  "We will save hundreds of lives," he said this week.  While most testing uses so-called PCR kits aimed at identifying the virus in patients, Slovakia will rely on cheaper antibody tests in an experiment closely watched by the 27-member European Union.  This week, the bloc's executive commission pledged to spend €100 million (S$159 million) for member states to purchase the tests, which are able to provide results within 15 minutes.  The two-day programme will start on Saturday (Oct 31) at 7am and be repeated a week later.  Anyone who doesn't take part must show proof of a negative test to be able to move around in public.  In the weekend pilot, about 5,600 people, or 4 per cent, were positive and were put into quarantine.  The true rate may be as much as three times higher, however, as the test fails to identify cases in people who haven't yet developed an immune response.  Medical experts have expressed scepticism, as the authorities won't follow up positive cases with PCR tests and contact tracing, while the initiative may exhaust an already stretched healthcare system.  A day before starting, officials were still seeking enough volunteer medical personnel to administer the tests.  Critics suggest it's aimed more at shoring up Mr Matovic's flagging popularity among voters than eradicating the virus.  President Zuzana Caputova found out about the plan when Mr Matovic announced it during a news conference.  By that time, his government had already purchased 13 million testing kits for about US$5 (S$7) each.  "This isn't an expert operation, but a political operation," said Dr Marian Kollar, the head of Slovakia's Medical Chamber.  The country "shouldn't take steps that don't have any significant impact on the spread of the pandemic".

predictions.text: Encouraged by turnout of more than 90 per cent at a pilot round last weekend, Prime Minister Igor Matovic has vowed to make Slovakia the world's first country to know the full scope of contagion in its borders. He has pushed the idea as an alternative to reimposing measures far harsher than in the spring, when lockdowns wiped out more than 10 per cent of most European economies.
predictions.reasoning: The prime minister vowed to test the entire population, showing acceptance of a bold plan.

Medical experts have expressed scepticism, as the authorities won't follow up positive cases with PCR tests and contact tracing, while the initiative may exhaust an already stretched healthcare system.
predictions.reasoning: Medical experts expressed skepticism about the mass testing plan, indicating rejection of its effectiveness.

results.actual_text: The Friday night lights of Texas high school football have taken on a mythical quality over the
results.plain_text: The Friday night lights of Texas high school football have taken on a mythical quality over the years, but Thursdays have a storied history of their own. Author Michael Hurd will take part in a live virtual event for the Allen Public Library to discuss the largely unknown history of Black high school football in the state. Hurd’s Thursday Night Lights: The Story of Black High School Football in Texas was named one of the 30 best Texas books for the 2010-19 decade by The Texas Observer and one of the 30 most essential books about Texas by the Houston Chronicle. The live event will be at 7:30 p.m. May 13 at allentx.swagit.com. Former Dallas Morning News columnist James Ragland will moderate the program. Former Dallas Cowboys receiver Tony Hill will also take part. Thursday Night Lights tells the inspiring story of Black high school football in Texas from 1920 to 1970. The segregated high schools in the Prairie View Interscholastic League (the Black counterpart to the University Interscholastic League) included future NFL greats such as “Mean” Joe Greene, Otis Taylor and Charles “Bubba” Smith, who appeared in six Police Academy movies after his All-Pro career. Hurd is also the author of Black College Football, 1892-1992, the only book that comprehensively documents the legacies of football programs at historically Black colleges, and he serves on the selection committee for the Black College Football Hall of Fame. He is a board member for the Writers’ League of Texas, a trustee with the Friends of the Texas Historical Commission and a member of the Texas Institute of Letters. For details about the virtual event, call 214-509-4911.

predictions.text: The segregated high schools in the Prairie View Interscholastic League (the Black counterpart to the University Interscholastic League) included future NFL greats such as “Mean” Joe Greene, Otis Taylor and Charles “Bubba” Smith, who appeared in six Police Academy movies after his All-Pro career.
predictions.reasoning: Mention of segregated high schools implies systemic rejection and denial of integration.
